Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n53\t\n54\t\n55\tdef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n56\t    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n57\t    try:\n58\t        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n59\t            content = f.read()\n60\t            if content.strip():\n61\t                return content\n62\t            else:\n63\t                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n64\t                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n65\t    except FileNotFoundError:\n66\t        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n67\t        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n68\t\n69\t\n70\t@dataclass\n71\tclass EscalateRequest:\n72\t    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n73\t    intent_id: str\n74\t    session_id: str\n75\t    utterance: str\n76\t    intent_type: str\n77\t    project_slug: str | None = None\n78\t    topic_id: str | None = None\n79\t    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n80\t    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n81\t\n82\t    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n83\t        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n84\t        return {\n85\t            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n86\t            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n87\t            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n88\t            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n89\t            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n90\t            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n91\t            \"context\": self.context,\n92\t            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n93\t        }\n94\t\n95\t\n96\t@dataclass\n97\tclass EscalateResult:\n98\t    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n99\t    bead_id: str\n100\t    intent_id: str\n101\t    pending_card: dict\n102\t    status: str = \"pending\"  # pending, created, failed\n103\t\n104\t    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n105\t        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n106\t        return {\n107\t            \"bead_id\": self.bead_id,\n108\t            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n109\t            \"pending_card\": self.pending_card,\n110\t            \"status\": self.status,\n111\t        }\n112\t\n113\t\n114\tclass EscalateError(Exception):\n115\t    \"\"\"Base exception for escalate errors.\"\"\"\n116\t    pass\n117\t\n118\t\n119\tclass BeadCreationError(EscalateError):\n120\t    \"\"\"Bead creation failed.\"\"\"\n121\t    pass\n122\t\n123\t\n124\tclass EscalateHandler:\n125\t    \"\"\"\n126\t    Escalate handler for task-profile intents.\n127\t\n128\t    Handles the full escalate flow:\n129\t    1. Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n130\t    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n131\t    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n132\t    \"\"\"\n133\t\n134\t    def __init__(self, store=None):\n135\t        self.store = store\n136\t        self._zai_client = None\n137\t        self._reload_manager = None\n138\t\n139\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n140\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n141\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n142\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n143\t        return self._zai_client\n144\t\n145\t    async def _get_store(self):\n146\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n147\t        if self.store is None:\n148\t            self.store = get_store()\n149\t        return self.store\n150\t\n151\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n152\t        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n153\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n154\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n155\t        return self._reload_manager\n156\t\n157\t    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n158\t        \"\"\"\n159\t        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n160\t\n161\t        Args:\n162\t            request: The escalate request\n163\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n164\t\n165\t        Returns:\n166\t            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n167\t        \"\"\"\n168\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n169\t\n170\t        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n171\t        context = {\n172\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n173\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n174\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n175\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n176\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n180\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n181\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n182\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n183\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n184\t\n185\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n186\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n187\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n188\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n189\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n190\t\n191\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n192\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n193\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n194\t\n195\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n196\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n197\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n198\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n199\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n200\t\n201\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n202\t            if action not in actions:\n203\t                continue\n204\t\n205\t            # Evaluate the condition\n206\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n207\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n208\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n209\t\n210\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n211\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n212\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n213\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n214\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n215\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n216\t\n217\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n218\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n219\t\n220\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n221\t        \"\"\"\n222\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n223\t\n224\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n225\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n226\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n227\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n228\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n229\t\n230\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n231\t\n232\t        Args:\n233\t            condition: The condition string\n234\t            context: Dict of available variables\n235\t\n236\t        Returns:\n237\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n238\t        \"\"\"\n239\t        try:\n240\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n241\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n242\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n243\t\n244\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n245\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n246\t            safe_locals = {\n247\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n248\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n249\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n250\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n251\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            # Evaluate condition\n255\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n256\t            return bool(result)\n257\t        except Exception as e:\n258\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n259\t            return False\n260\t\n261\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n262\t        \"\"\"\n263\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n264\t\n265\t        Args:\n266\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n267\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n268\t\n269\t        Returns:\n270\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n271\t        \"\"\"\n272\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n273\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n274\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n275\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n276\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n277\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n281\t        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n282\t\n283\t        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n284\t        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n285\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n286\t\n287\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n288\t        \"\"\"\n289\t        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n290\t\n291\t        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n292\t        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n293\t\n294\t        Args:\n295\t            request: The escalate request\n296\t\n297\t        Returns:\n298\t            Execution result dict\n299\t        \"\"\"\n300\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n301\t        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n302\t\n303\t        try:\n304\t            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n305\t            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n306\t                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n307\t            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n308\t                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n309\t            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n310\t                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n311\t            else:\n312\t                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n313\t                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n314\t                return {\n315\t                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n316\t                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n317\t                    \"data\": {\n318\t                        \"action\": action,\n319\t                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n320\t                    },\n321\t                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n322\t                }\n323\t\n324\t        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n326\t            return {\n327\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n328\t                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n329\t                \"data\": {\n330\t                    \"action\": action,\n331\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n332\t                },\n333\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n334\t            }\n335\t        except Exception as e:\n336\t            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n337\t            return {\n338\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n339\t                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n340\t                \"data\": {\n341\t                    \"action\": action,\n342\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n343\t                },\n344\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n345\t            }\n346\t\n347\t    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n348\t        \"\"\"\n349\t        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n350\t\n351\t        Args:\n352\t            request: The escalate request\n353\t\n354\t        Returns:\n355\t            Execution result dict\n356\t        \"\"\"\n357\t        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n358\t\n359\t        # Execute the delete command\n366\t        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n367\t            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n368\t            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n369\t            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n370\t        )\n371\t\n372\t        return result\n373\t\n374\t    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n375\t        \"\"\"\n376\t        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n377\t\n378\t        Args:\n379\t            request: The escalate request\n380\t\n381\t        Returns:\n382\t            Execution result dict\n383\t        \"\"\"\n384\t        # Build context for the LLM\n431\t        context_parts = [\n432\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n433\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n434\t        ]\n435\t\n436\t        if request.project_slug:\n437\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n438\t\n439\t        if request.topic_id:\n440\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n441\t\n442\t        # Add any additional context\n443\t        if request.context:\n444\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n445\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n446\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Build the user message\n449\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n450\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n451\t            \"\",\n452\t            *context_parts,\n453\t            \"\",\n454\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n455\t        ])\n456\t\n457\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t        # Make LLM call\n460\t        try:\n461\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n462\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n463\t                user_message=user_message,\n464\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n465\t                max_tokens=4096,\n466\t                temperature=0.7,\n467\t            )\n468\t\n469\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n470\t            return bead_body\n471\t\n472\t        except Exception as e:\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n474\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n475\t\n476\t    async def create_bead(\n477\t        self,\n478\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n479\t        bead_body: str,\n480\t    ) -> str:\n481\t        \"\"\"\n482\t        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n483\t\n484\t        Returns the bead ID.\n485\t        \"\"\"\n486\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n487\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n488\t\n489\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n490\t        metadata = {\n491\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n492\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n493\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n494\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n495\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n496\t        }\n497\t\n498\t        if request.project_slug:\n499\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n500\t\n501\t        if request.topic_id:\n502\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n503\t\n504\t        # Build br create command\n505\t        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n506\t        # Note: br uses --description for the main body content\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n509\t            cmd = [\n510\t                \"br\",\n511\t                \"create\",\n512\t                \"--title\", title,\n513\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n514\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n515\t            ]\n516\t\n517\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n518\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n519\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n520\t                if value is not None:\n521\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n522\t\n523\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n524\t                *cmd,\n525\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n526\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t            )\n528\t\n529\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n530\t\n531\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n532\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n533\t                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n534\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t\n536\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n537\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n538\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n539\t\n540\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n541\t            return bead_id\n542\t\n543\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n544\t            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n545\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n546\t        except Exception as e:\n547\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n548\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n549\t\n550\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n551\t        self,\n552\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n553\t        bead_body: str,\n554\t        bead_type: str,\n555\t    ) -> str:\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the br CLI.\n558\t\n559\t        Returns the bead ID.\n560\t        \"\"\"\n561\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n562\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n563\t\n564\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n565\t        metadata = {\n566\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n567\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n568\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n569\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n570\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        if request.project_slug:\n574\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n575\t\n576\t        if request.topic_id:\n577\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n578\t\n579\t        # Build br create command\n580\t        try:\n581\t            cmd = [\n582\t                \"br\",\n583\t                \"create\",\n584\t                \"--title\", title,\n585\t                \"--type\", bead_type,\n586\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n587\t            ]\n588\t\n589\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n590\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n591\t                if value is not None:\n592\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n593\t\n594\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n595\t                *cmd,\n596\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n597\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n598\t            )\n599\t\n600\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n601\t\n602\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n603\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n604\t                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n605\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n606\t\n607\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n608\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n609\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n610\t\n611\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n612\t            return bead_id\n613\t\n614\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n615\t            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n616\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n617\t        except Exception as e:\n618\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n619\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n620\t\n621\t    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n622\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n623\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n624\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n625\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n626\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n627\t        else:\n628\t            title = utterance\n629\t\n630\t        # Add project prefix if available\n631\t        if request.project_slug:\n632\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n633\t\n634\t        return title\n635\t\n636\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n637\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from br create output.\n638\t\n639\t        br create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n640\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n641\t        \"\"\"\n642\t        import re\n643\t\n644\t        # Strip whitespace first\n645\t        output = output.strip()\n646\t\n647\t        if not output:\n648\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty br create output\")\n649\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n650\t\n651\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n652\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n653\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n654\t        patterns = [\n655\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n656\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n657\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n658\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n659\t        ]\n660\t\n661\t        for pattern in patterns:\n662\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n663\t            if matches:\n664\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n665\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n666\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n667\t                return bead_id\n668\t\n669\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n670\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n671\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n672\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n673\t            return output\n674\t\n675\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (br might have changed format)\n676\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected br create output format: {output}\")\n677\t        return output\n678\t\n679\t    def build_pending_card(\n680\t        self,\n681\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n682\t        bead_id: str,\n683\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n684\t    ) -> dict:\n685\t        \"\"\"\n686\t        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n687\t\n688\t        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n689\t        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n690\t        \"\"\"\n691\t        return {\n692\t            \"type\": \"pending\",\n693\t            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n694\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n695\t            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n696\t            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n697\t            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n698\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n699\t            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n700\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n701\t            \"metadata\": {\n702\t                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n703\t                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n704\t                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n705\t            },\n706\t        }\n707\t\n708\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n709\t        \"\"\"\n710\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n711\t\n712\t        Full flow:\n713\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n714\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n715\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n716\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n717\t\n718\t        Args:\n719\t            request: The escalate request\n720\t\n721\t        Returns:\n722\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n723\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n724\t        \"\"\"\n725\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n726\t\n727\t        try:\n728\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n729\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n730\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n731\t\n732\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n733\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n734\t\n735\t            if auto_approve:\n736\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n737\t\n738\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n739\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n740\t\n741\t                # Update intent in store\n742\t                store = await self._get_store()\n743\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n744\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n745\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n746\t                )\n747\t\n748\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n749\t                return EscalateResult(\n750\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n751\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n752\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n753\t                    status=\"completed\",\n754\t                )\n755\t\n756\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n757\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n758\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n759\t\n760\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n761\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n762\t\n763\t            # Step 5: Create bead with determined self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n768\t\n769\t            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n770\t            store = await self._get_store()\n771\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n772\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n773\t                status=\"dispatched\",\n774\t            )\n775\t\n776\t            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n777\t\n778\t            return EscalateResult(\n779\t                bead_id=bead_id,\n780\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n781\t                pending_card=pending_card,\n782\t                status=\"created\",\n783\t            )\n784\t\n785\t        except EscalateError:\n786\t            raise\n787\t        except Exception as e:\n788\t            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n789\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n790\t\n791\t\n792\t# Global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n798\t    global _handler\n799\t    if _handler is None:\n800\t        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n801\t    return _handler\n802\t\n803\t\n804\tasync def Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Captures the full scope of the request\n2. Includes relevant context (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. Defines clear success criteria\n4. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            if content.strip():\n                return content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  # pending, created, failed\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"bead_id\": self.bead_id,\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"pending_card\": self.pending_card,\n            \"status\": self.status,\n        }\n\n\nclass EscalateError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for escalate errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass BeadCreationError(EscalateError):\n    \"\"\"Bead creation failed.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate handler for task-profile intents.\n\n    Handles the full escalate flow:\n    1. Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n            else:\n                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n                return {\n                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"action\": action,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                }\n\n        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n\n    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n\n        # Parse utterance to extract pod name and namespace\n        params = executor.parse_delete_pod_utterance(\n            utterance=request.utterance,\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        # Execute the delete command\n        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement git commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Git command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: br uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from br create output.\n\n        br create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty br create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (br might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected br create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n","numLines":816,"startLine":1,"totalLines":816}}}
